title: Catalog matching joins
text: coord_inner_join() keeps rows x matches y within max_sep. coord_left_join() keeps rows x; unmatched rows get NA y. coord_right_join() keeps rows y; unmatched rows get NA x. coord_full_join() keeps rows tables. coord_nearest_join() returns one nearest match y row x. multiple = "" keeps matches inside radius, multiple = "closest" keeps one match per row x.

title: Catalog matching joins
code: set.seed(1) N <- 10 df1 <- data.frame(   ra = runif(N),    dec = runif(N),   cat = "first" ) df2 <- data.frame(   ra = runif(N),    dec = runif(N),   cat = "second" )  df12 <- do.call(rbind, list(df1, df2)) df1$sc <- ra_dec(df1$ra, df1$dec) df2$sc <- ra_dec(df2$ra, df2$dec)  df12$sc <- c(df1$sc, df2$sc)  p1 <- df12 |>   ggplot(aes(ra, dec, color = cat)) +   geom_point() +   coord_fixed(ratio = 1) p1

title: Catalog matching joins
code: df12_intersect <- df1 |>   coord_inner_join(df2, max_sep = 5, unit = "arcmin")  p1 +   geom_segment(     data = df12_intersect,     aes(x = ra.x, xend = ra.y, y = dec.x, yend = dec.y),     color = "black"   ) +   ggforce::geom_circle(     data = df12_intersect,     aes(x0 = ra.x, y0 = dec.x, r= 5/60),     fill = "grey50",     alpha = 0.2,     inherit.aes = FALSE   ) +     geom_label(       data = df12_intersect,       aes(ra.x, dec.x + 0.1, label = paste(round(sep, 1), "arcmin")),       inherit.aes = FALSE,           )

title: Catalog matching joins
code: df12_nearest <- df1 |>   coord_nearest_join(df2, unit = "arcmin")   p1 +   geom_segment(     data = df12_nearest,     aes(x = ra.x, xend = ra.y, y = dec.x, yend = dec.y),     color = "black"   ) +   ggforce::geom_circle(     data = df12_nearest,     aes(x0 = ra.x, y0 = dec.x, r = sep/60),     fill = "grey50",     alpha = 0.2,     inherit.aes = FALSE   ) +     geom_label(       data = df12_nearest,       aes(ra.x, dec.x + 0.1, label = paste(round(sep, 1), "arcmin")),       inherit.aes = FALSE     )

what: Left vs right vs full join
title: Catalog matching joins
Catalog matching joins
code: set.seed(1)  N <- 13 df1 <- data.frame(   ra = runif(N),    dec = runif(N),   cat = "first" ) df2 <- data.frame(   ra = runif(N),    dec = runif(N),   cat = "second" )  df12 <- do.call(rbind, list(df1, df2)) df1$sc <- ra_dec(df1$ra, df1$dec) df2$sc <- ra_dec(df2$ra, df2$dec)  df12$sc <- c(df1$sc, df2$sc)  p1 <- df12 |>   ggplot(aes(ra, dec, color = cat)) +   geom_point() +   coord_fixed(ratio = 1) p1

title: Catalog matching joins
code: MAX_SEP <- 8 df12_intersect <- df1 |>   coord_inner_join(df2, max_sep = MAX_SEP, unit = "arcmin")  p1 +   geom_segment(     data = df12_intersect,     aes(x = ra.x, xend = ra.y, y = dec.x, yend = dec.y),     color = "black"   ) +   ggforce::geom_circle(     data = df12_intersect,     aes(x0 = ra.x, y0 = dec.x, r = MAX_SEP/60),     fill = "grey50",     alpha = 0.2,     inherit.aes = FALSE   )

title: Catalog matching joins
code: df12_intersect <- df1 |>   coord_inner_join(df2, max_sep = MAX_SEP, unit = "arcmin", multiple = "closest")  p1 +   geom_segment(     data = df12_intersect,     aes(x = ra.x, xend = ra.y, y = dec.x, yend = dec.y),     color = "black"   ) +   ggforce::geom_circle(     data = df12_intersect,     aes(x0 = ra.x, y0 = dec.x, r= MAX_SEP/60),     fill = "grey50",     alpha = 0.2,     inherit.aes = FALSE   )

code: @Manual{,   title = {astrocoords: Astronomical Coordinates for R},   author = {Grigory Uskov},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.1},   url = {https://uskovgs.github.io/astrocoords/}, }

title: Astronomical Coordinates for R
text: astrocoords R package basic astronomical coordinate time conversion. Core coordinate time calculations astrocoords based ERFA library. currently supports: ICRS Galactic sky coordinates angular separation sphere simple frame transformation ICRS Galactic coordinate matching catalogs (coord_match(), coord_nearest()), including kdtree method spatial join helpers data.frames (coord_left_join(), coord_nearest_join(), coord_inner_join(), etc.) sexagesimal conversion helpers (deg_to_hms(), deg_to_dms(), hms_to_deg(), dms_to_deg()) Julian Date / Modified Julian Date conversion

title: Astronomical Coordinates for R
text: can install development version astrocoords GitHub :

title: Astronomical Coordinates for R
text: Create coordinates ICRS frame: Transform Galactic coordinates: Measure angular separation arcseconds: Find nearest matches two coordinate vectors: Parse compact coordinate string: Work Julian Date:
code: library(astrocoords) #>  #> Attaching package: 'astrocoords' #> The following object is masked from 'package:graphics': #>  #>     frame  x <- ra_dec(c(10, 20), c(30, 40)) x #> <sky|icrs>[2] #> [1] 00h40m00.0s +30°00'00" 01h20m00.0s +40°00'00" transform_to(x, galactic()) #> <sky|galactic>[2] #> [1] +119°59'08" -32°48'23" +128°50'59" -22°32'36" separation(ra_dec(10, 20), ra_dec(11, 21)) #> [1] 4932.552 coord_nearest(   ra_dec(c(10, 20), c(0, 0)),   ra_dec(c(10.001, 19.999), c(0, 0)),   unit = "arcsec",   method = "kdtree" ) #>   x_id y_id sep #> 1    1    1 3.6 #> 2    2    2 3.6 parse_coord("J230631.0+155633") #> <sky|icrs>[1] #> [1] 23h06m31.0s +15°56'33" jd <- datetime_to_jd(as.POSIXct("2026-03-08 12:34:56", tz = "UTC")) jd #> [1] 2461108  jd_to_datetime(jd, tz = "UTC") #> [1] "2026-03-08 12:34:56 UTC"

what: Spatial left join for sky coordinates — coord_left_join
title: Spatial left join for sky coordinates — coord_left_join
text: Join two tables angular distance `<sky_coord>` columns.

title: Spatial left join for sky coordinates — coord_left_join
code: coord_left_join(   x,   y,   x_coord = NULL,   y_coord = NULL,   max_sep,   unit = "arcsec",   method = "kdtree",   multiple = "all",   keep_sep = TRUE,   sep_col = "sep",   suffix = c(".x", ".y") )  coord_nearest_join(   x,   y,   x_coord = NULL,   y_coord = NULL,   unit = "arcsec",   method = "kdtree",   keep_sep = TRUE,   sep_col = "sep",   suffix = c(".x", ".y") )  coord_right_join(   x,   y,   x_coord = NULL,   y_coord = NULL,   max_sep,   unit = "arcsec",   method = "kdtree",   multiple = "all",   keep_sep = TRUE,   sep_col = "sep",   suffix = c(".x", ".y") )  coord_full_join(   x,   y,   x_coord = NULL,   y_coord = NULL,   max_sep,   unit = "arcsec",   method = "kdtree",   multiple = "all",   keep_sep = TRUE,   sep_col = "sep",   suffix = c(".x", ".y") )  coord_inner_join(   x,   y,   x_coord = NULL,   y_coord = NULL,   max_sep,   unit = "arcsec",   method = "kdtree",   multiple = "all",   keep_sep = TRUE,   sep_col = "sep",   suffix = c(".x", ".y") )

title: Spatial left join for sky coordinates — coord_left_join
text: x, y Data frames (tibble-like objects). x_coord, y_coord Coordinate column `x`/`y`. Supports bare names character names. `NULL`, first `<sky_coord>` column auto-detected. max_sep Maximum separation threshold passed [coord_match()]. Used [coord_left_join()]. [coord_nearest_join()] always uses `Inf`. unit Separation unit passed [coord_match()]: `"arcsec"`, `"arcmin"`, `"deg"`, `"rad"`. method Matching backend passed [coord_match()]: `"kdtree"` `"bruteforce"`. multiple handle multiple matches per row `x`: `""` keeps matches, `"closest"` keeps one nearest match per `x` row. Used [coord_left_join()]. keep_sep `TRUE`, append separation column output. sep_col Name separation column output. suffix Length-2 character vector duplicate non-key column names.

title: Spatial left join for sky coordinates — coord_left_join
text: joined table. Currently returned base `data.frame`.

title: Match two sky catalogs within angular radius — coord_match
text: x, y `<sky_coord>` vectors. max_sep Maximum separation threshold. Used [coord_match()]. [coord_nearest()] always uses `Inf`. unit Separation unit: `"arcsec"`, `"arcmin"`, `"deg"`, `"rad"`. method Matching backend: `"kdtree"` `"bruteforce"`.

title: Match two sky catalogs within angular radius — coord_match
text: Base `data.frame` columns `x_id`, `y_id`, `sep`.   `sep` returned unit `unit`.

title: Match two sky catalogs within angular radius — coord_match
text: frames differ, `y` transformed frame `x`. Rows sorted `x_id`, `sep`, `y_id`. `x_id` matches, one row returned `y_id = NA` `sep = NA`.

title: Convert date-time to Julian Date (JD) — datetime_to_jd
Convert date-time to Julian Date (JD) — datetime_to_jd
text: x Date-time values `POSIXct`, `POSIXlt`, `Date`. `Date` values treated midnight (`00:00:00`) UTC.

title: Convert date-time to Julian Date (JD) — datetime_to_jd
text: Numeric vector Julian Date values (days).

title: Convert date-time to Julian Date (JD) — datetime_to_jd
text: JD continuous day count used astronomy. fractional part time day. JD starts noon, midnight shown `.5`. Uses ERFA `scale = "UTC"`. Leap seconds handled ERFA.

title: Convert date-time to Julian Date (JD) — datetime_to_jd
code: old_accuracy <- getOption("digits") options(digits = 11)  t <- as.POSIXct("2026-03-08 12:34:56", tz = "UTC") datetime_to_jd(t) #> [1] 2461108.0243  datetime_to_jd(as.Date("2026-03-08")) #> [1] 2461107.5  options(digits = old_accuracy)

title: Build IAU-style source names from sky coordinates — iau_name
code: x <- parse_coord("J230631.0+155633")  iau_name(x) #> [1] "J230631.0+155633" iau_name(x, prefix = "SRGA J") #> [1] "SRGA J230631.0+155633" iau_name(x, ra_digits = 2, dec_digits = 1) #> [1] "J230631.00+155633.0"  # Non-ICRS inputs are transformed internally iau_name(transform_to(x, galactic())) #> [1] "J230631.0+155633"

what: Parse coordinate strings into ICRS sky_coord — parse_coord
title: Parse coordinate strings into ICRS sky_coord — parse_coord
text: Parse character vector ICRS coordinates `sky_coord` vector. parser currently supports two input families:

title: Parse coordinate strings into ICRS sky_coord — parse_coord
Parse coordinate strings into ICRS sky_coord — parse_coord
text: x Character vector ICRS coordinates. `NA` values preserved.

title: Parse coordinate strings into ICRS sky_coord — parse_coord
text: <sky_coord> vector ICRS frame.

title: Parse coordinate strings into ICRS sky_coord — parse_coord
text: 1. Compact J-style token (can appear inside catalog names):    - `JHHMMSS(.s)+DDMMSS(.s)`    - examples: `"J230631.0+155633"`, `"SRGA J230631.0+155633"` 2. Sexagesimal token full components:    - `HH MM SS +/-DD MM SS`    - six components required (partial forms)    - separators may spaces, `:`, HMS/DMS markers (`h m s d \u00B0 ' "`)    - examples: `"12 34 56 -76 54 3.210"`,      `"12:34:56 -76:54:3.210"`,      `"12h34m56s -76d54m3.210s"`

what: Set printing style for sky coordinates — set_print
title: Set printing style for sky coordinates — set_print
text: helpers control `sky_coord` objects displayed printed formatted.

title: Set printing style for sky coordinates — set_print
Set printing style for sky coordinates — set_print
text: Invisibly returns previous notation.

title: Set printing style for sky coordinates — set_print
text: `set_print_hms()` uses sexagesimal notation `00h40m00.0s +20d00'00"`. `set_print_plain()` uses compact plain-text style `00 40 000.0 +20 00 00`. `set_print_colon()` uses compact colon style `00:40:00.0 +20:00:00`. `set_print_pair()` prints decimal degree pairs `(10, 20)`. current style stored `options(astrocoords.notation = ...)`.

title: Set printing style for sky coordinates — set_print
code: x <- ra_dec(10, 20)  set_print_hms() format(x) #> [1] "00h40m00.0s +20°00'00\""  set_print_plain() format(x) #> [1] "00 40 00.0 +20 00 00"  set_print_colon() format(x) #> [1] "00:40:00.0 +20:00:00"  set_print_pair() format(x) #> [1] "(10, 20)"

title: Create sky coordinates — sky_coord
text: lon Longitude-like coordinate degrees. `frame = icrs()`, right ascension (`ra`) must   `0` `360`. `frame = galactic()`, Galactic longitude (`l`) must   `0` `360`. lat Latitude-like coordinate degrees. `frame = icrs()`, declination (`dec`) must   `-90` `90`. `frame = galactic()`, Galactic latitude (`b`) must   `-90` `90`. frame sky frame object. Supported frames icrs() galactic(). default icrs().

Transform sky coordinates to another frame — transform_to
text: x <sky_coord> vector. frame Target frame. Use icrs() galactic().

title: Transform sky coordinates to another frame — transform_to
text: transformed <sky_coord> vector.

title: Transform sky coordinates to another frame — transform_to
text: Supported transformations : icrs() -> galactic() galactic() -> icrs() x already requested frame, returned unchanged. icrs() <-> galactic() conversion, ERFA documentation states matrix-element precision 20 microarcseconds. Use transform_to() (transform()) sky_coord objects.

title: Transform sky coordinates to another frame — transform_to
code: x <- ra_dec(c(10, 120), c(20, -30)) x #> <sky|icrs>[2] #> [1] (10, 20)   (120, -30)  g <- transform_to(x, galactic()) g #> <sky|galactic>[2] #> [1] (119.2694, -42.79039515) (247.0829, -0.04805424)   transform_to(g, icrs()) #> <sky|icrs>[2] #> [1] (10, 20)   (120, -30)  # Pipe-friendly workflow ra_dec(10, 20) |>   transform_to(galactic()) |>   transform_to(icrs()) #> <sky|icrs>[1] #> [1] (10, 20)
